methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Endurance Energy is a Seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector, including advanced-stage companies such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage step-ups, implying a valuation of $12M. The peer-median approach benchmarks against three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). This peer cohort yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range of $110M–$200M. The significant gap between these two methods reflects the contrast between stage-appropriate dilution patterns and later-stage peer benchmarks.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $11M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $81M. This wide spread indicates material uncertainty around whether Endurance Energy should be valued using early-stage progression mechanics or whether its technology and market position justify valuation at the lower end of Series A peer multiples. Refinement would require clarity on product-market fit, capital intensity relative to peers, and time-to-Series A.
